content.assessment: This proposal does not draw directly on any of the supplied international-comparison findings for its core structural mechanism (grade-split), instead citing Polish reform findings from outside this lens's evidence set. Within the lens evidence supplied, the closest relevant analogy is the general caution about depopulation dynamics (F-live-international_comparison-01) and the reminder that most policy literature is European and untested for qualitative/contextual validity outside that scope (F-live-international_comparison-10). No supplied finding directly evaluates grade-split consolidation models internationally, limiting this lens's ability to assess comparability or transferability with confidence.
opportunities: If evidence on multigrade retention proximity effects were incorporated more directly from this lens (e.g., depopulation acceleration findings), it could strengthen the rationale for lower-grade retention specifically
strengths: The underlying concern about young children benefiting from proximity is consistent with cross-national concern about school-closure effects on local retention (F-live-international_comparison-01), lending indirect plausibility to preserving early grades locally
threats: Absence of directly comparable international precedent within this lens's evidence increases risk that the structural model's transferability is asserted rather than demonstrated

The depopulation-acceleration finding (F-live-international_comparison-01) applies to closures generally; if the grade-split still results in effective closure of upper grades locally, similar acceleration risk may apply, undermining the model's protective rationale
weaknesses: No supplied international-comparison finding directly documents or evaluates a grade-split structural model comparable to the one proposed, so comparability cannot be established from this evidence set alone

The systematic review's finding that policy transferability evidence is confined to European contexts and quantitative depopulation metrics (F-live-international_comparison-10) means even adjacent European evidence used elsewhere in the proposal cannot be assumed valid for judging qualitative schooling-structure effects
